_note: Synthetic example demonstrating UGR failure mode in drawing-office context. 24 LED_PANEL_600 panels in 4×6 grid (uniform layout — lumen-method PASSes; uniformity PASSes); but room_type=drawing_office tightens UGR limit to 16 per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34, and the general-office UGR-class opal panels produce max UGR 17.5 > 16 limit. INV-03 FAIL HIGH; INV-01 + INV-02 PASS. 10-sample slice from the 108-point grid; full grid emitted by runtime calc.lumen_grid_solver.

consumed_summary._layout_note: 24 panels in 4×6 grid: x ∈ {1500, 4000, 6500, 9000, 10500} approximate; y ∈ {1500, 4500, 7500} approximate, uniform spacing fitted to 12×9 m room. Note: the panels are general-office UGR-class (≤19); not the stricter drawing-office UGR-class (≤16) that this room_type requires.

ies_files._source: Synthetic 600x600mm recessed LED panel, 6000lm, 4000K, opal diffuser. General-office UGR-class panel. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — generated by scripts/generate_reference_ies.py. Used to demonstrate per-room-type UGR mismatch detection in drawing-office context.

grid_metadata.grid_spacing_formula: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 adaptive: p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(d) clamped [50, 1000] mm; for d=11.0m task area: p ≈ 0.2 × 5^1.041 × 1000 ≈ 1062 mm → clamped to MAX 1000 mm. Grid fitted to task area: 12 cols × 9 rows = 108 points (x-step 1000, y-step 1000 fits 11000×8000 mm task-area extent).

reflectances._source: Default drawing-office reflectances per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 clean office (0.7/0.5/0.2); same as open_plan_office default.

non_compliance_flags.message: Max UGR 17.5 (auto_S_wall observer at (6000, 1500)) exceeds drawing-office limit 16 per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34. The luminaires in upstream intent are general-office UGR-class (≤19) opal panels; drawing-office work requires UGR-class-rated luminaires (UGR ≤ 16) with batwing or asymmetric distribution that limits direct view of high-luminance surfaces from the seated observer position. Recommend specifying a UGR≤16 panel (e.g. micro-prism or louvred specular reflector type) and re-running the cascade.
non_compliance_flags.reference: BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.3 row 5.34 (drawing-office UGR limit) + CIE 117 (UGR method)

rationale.chat_summary: Synthetic UGR failure demo for drawing-office context. 12×9 m drawing_office with 24 LED_PANEL_600 (UGR-class ≤19 general-office panels) in uniform 4×6 grid. Lumen-method targets PASS (avg 762 lux ≥ 750 target; U₀ 0.78 ≥ 0.7 drawing-office target), but room_type=drawing_office triggers ugr_limit=16 per Table 5.3 row 5.34, and max UGR 17.5 (auto_S_wall) exceeds the limit. INV-01 + INV-02 + INV-04..09 PASS; INV-03 FAIL HIGH. Reviewer D-3 flag fires: substitute UGR-class-rated luminaires (UGR ≤ 16) with batwing or asymmetric distribution.

sections.title: Cascade context
sections.summary: Consumes synthetic upstream lighting-layout intent (./synthetic-upstream-intent.json) — drawing-office room_type with general-office UGR-class luminaires. Constructed to exercise the INV-03 FAIL path that fires when per-room-type UGR limit tightens from the default 19.

sections.title: Grid resolution derivation
sections.summary: Task area 11×8 m (12×9 m room minus 500 mm perimeter border). d=11m; corrected §6.2 formula p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(11) × 1000 ≈ 0.2 × 5.31 × 1000 ≈ 1062 mm CLAMPED to max 1000 mm per [grid-spacing-rules#adaptive-formula]. 12 cols × 9 rows = 108 points.

sections.title: Per-point illuminance
sections.summary: achieved_avg 762 lux ≥ 750 target (BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34 drawing-office target); achieved_min 598 lux ≥ 0.7×750 = 525 PASS INV-01; achieved_uniformity_u0 0.78 ≥ 0.7 drawing-office target PASS INV-02. The layout-level illuminance + uniformity is engineered correctly; the failure is in luminaire selection, not layout geometry.

sections.title: UGR per CIE 117 + per-room-type mismatch
sections.summary: 4 default observers; max UGR 17.5 (auto_S_wall) > 16 drawing-office limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34. The opal-diffuser Lambertian distribution from the synthetic LED_PANEL_600 IES has high luminance in the 50–60° elevation band (the glare-relevant angles per CIE 117), which the drawing-office UGR limit specifically forbids. UGR-class-rated panels (UGR ≤ 16) use micro-prism or louvred reflector optics to cut the 50–60° emission below the CIE 117 glare threshold.

sections.title: Engineering signal
sections.summary: Engineer-of-record sees task_area_compliant=false propagated upstream to lighting-layout INV-11; reviewer D-3 flag fires (D-3 UGR-class mismatch with per-room-type requirement). Required action: substitute the LED_PANEL_600 (general-office, UGR ≤19) with a UGR-class panel rated UGR ≤ 16 — typically a micro-prism or louvred specular-reflector type. After substitution, the cascade re-runs and max UGR drops into the 14–15 range.

sections.title: Honest disclosure
sections.summary: Synthetic upstream intent + synthetic IES file; this example is a skill self-test demonstration of the per-room-type UGR-mismatch path, not a real-project case. The 17.5 max UGR value is a runtime-calc-computable result on the geometry + Lambertian IES; the engineering interpretation (UGR-class substitution) is the actionable signal photometric-analysis adds on top of lumen-method.

invariants.evidence: Achieved min 598 lux ≥ 0.7 × target 750 = 525 lux per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34 drawing_office + §4.4. Cold point at (11500, 8500) SE corner; PASS by 73 lux margin.
Achieved U₀ 598/762 = 0.785 ≥ 0.7 drawing-office target per [ugr-rules#per-room-type-limits] drawing_office. PASS by 0.085 margin; uniform 4×6 grid produces good spatial distribution.

Max UGR 17.5 (auto_S_wall observer at (6000, 1500)) > 16 drawing-office limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34 drawing_office. FAIL by 1.5 UGR units. Cause: general-office UGR-class opal panel (UGR ≤19) used in a drawing-office context that requires UGR ≤16 luminaires.
1 distinct luminaire_type (LED_PANEL_600) in upstream synthetic intent consumed_summary; 1 ies_files[] entry matches; LM-63 parser returns total_lumens=6000 + max_intensity_cd=1850.
grid_spacing_mm=1000 matches BS EN 12464-1 §6.2 formula clamped output: corrected formula gives p ≈ 1062 mm clamped to max 1000 mm. d=11.0m task area.
illuminance_grid.length (full runtime emission) = point_count=108. 10-sample slice in this IR; every (x_mm, y_mm) inside task_area_bounds [500,11500]×[500,8500]. No duplicate coordinates.

4 ugr_results entries with _source=cie_117_default (N, S, E, W wall-facing per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions]). No engineer overrides supplied; would land in inputs.ugr_view_positions_override[] for specific drafting stations.
ies_files[0]._source length=259 chars ≥ 40 minimum; verification_status=synthetic_reference_C3 in enum per [ies-provenance-rules#verification-status-enum].

tool_call_pending=false; _calc_tool='calc.lumen_grid_solver'; _calc_engine_version='calc.lumen_grid_solver 1.0'. Runtime cascade completed successfully — the calc ran; the result is non-compliant by design (UGR-class mismatch).
